# LazyArray { #mertisreader.LazyArray }

```python
LazyArray(fits_data, header=None, hdu=None)
```

Lazy-loading proxy for FITS data arrays using memmap.

Per ADR-006:
- Defer I/O via astropy.io.fits.open(..., memmap=True)
- .shape, .dtype, .header never trigger I/O
- Safe: slicing, iteration, .mean(), .sum()
- Unsafe (raise NotImplementedError): fancy indexing, reshape, transpose
- .materialize() forces full load into RAM

## Attributes

| Name | Description |
| --- | --- |
| [dtype](#mertisreader.LazyArray.dtype) | Array data type. No I/O triggered. |
| [header](#mertisreader.LazyArray.header) | FITS header. No I/O triggered. |
| [materialized](#mertisreader.LazyArray.materialized) | Whether array has been materialized into RAM. |
| [ndim](#mertisreader.LazyArray.ndim) | Number of dimensions. No I/O triggered. |
| [shape](#mertisreader.LazyArray.shape) | Array shape. No I/O triggered. |
| [size](#mertisreader.LazyArray.size) | Total number of elements. No I/O triggered. |

## Methods

| Name | Description |
| --- | --- |
| [astype](#mertisreader.LazyArray.astype) | Cast array to specified dtype - works on memmap data. |
| [copy](#mertisreader.LazyArray.copy) | Create a copy of the array - triggers materialization. |
| [flatten](#mertisreader.LazyArray.flatten) | Flatten array - requires materialization. |
| [materialize](#mertisreader.LazyArray.materialize) | Force full array load into RAM and return a copy. |
| [max](#mertisreader.LazyArray.max) | Compute maximum - works on memmap data. |
| [mean](#mertisreader.LazyArray.mean) | Compute mean - works on memmap data without full load. |
| [min](#mertisreader.LazyArray.min) | Compute minimum - works on memmap data. |
| [ravel](#mertisreader.LazyArray.ravel) | Flatten array - requires materialization. |
| [reshape](#mertisreader.LazyArray.reshape) | Reshape array - requires materialization. |
| [std](#mertisreader.LazyArray.std) | Compute standard deviation - works on memmap data. |
| [sum](#mertisreader.LazyArray.sum) | Compute sum - works on memmap data without full load. |
| [swapaxes](#mertisreader.LazyArray.swapaxes) | Swap axes - requires materialization. |
| [transpose](#mertisreader.LazyArray.transpose) | Transpose array - requires materialization. |

### astype { #mertisreader.LazyArray.astype }

```python
LazyArray.astype(dtype)
```

Cast array to specified dtype - works on memmap data.

### copy { #mertisreader.LazyArray.copy }

```python
LazyArray.copy()
```

Create a copy of the array - triggers materialization.

### flatten { #mertisreader.LazyArray.flatten }

```python
LazyArray.flatten()
```

Flatten array - requires materialization.

### materialize { #mertisreader.LazyArray.materialize }

```python
LazyArray.materialize()
```

Force full array load into RAM and return a copy.

### max { #mertisreader.LazyArray.max }

```python
LazyArray.max(axis=None, dtype=None, out=None)
```

Compute maximum - works on memmap data.

### mean { #mertisreader.LazyArray.mean }

```python
LazyArray.mean(axis=None, dtype=None, out=None)
```

Compute mean - works on memmap data without full load.

### min { #mertisreader.LazyArray.min }

```python
LazyArray.min(axis=None, dtype=None, out=None)
```

Compute minimum - works on memmap data.

### ravel { #mertisreader.LazyArray.ravel }

```python
LazyArray.ravel()
```

Flatten array - requires materialization.

### reshape { #mertisreader.LazyArray.reshape }

```python
LazyArray.reshape(*args, **kwargs)
```

Reshape array - requires materialization.

### std { #mertisreader.LazyArray.std }

```python
LazyArray.std(axis=None, dtype=None, out=None)
```

Compute standard deviation - works on memmap data.

### sum { #mertisreader.LazyArray.sum }

```python
LazyArray.sum(axis=None, dtype=None, out=None)
```

Compute sum - works on memmap data without full load.

### swapaxes { #mertisreader.LazyArray.swapaxes }

```python
LazyArray.swapaxes(axis1, axis2)
```

Swap axes - requires materialization.

### transpose { #mertisreader.LazyArray.transpose }

```python
LazyArray.transpose(*args)
```

Transpose array - requires materialization.
